title: claude_credentials::CLAUDE_CODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT constant tightly couples openclaudia identity to upstream API check
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
decoupling
## Location
src/claude_credentials.rs:378-423 (CLAUDE_CODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT, inject_system_prompt)
## Violation
```rust
pub const CLAUDE_CODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT: &str =
    "You are Claude Code, Anthropic's official CLI for Claude.";
...
let prefix_block = serde_json::json!({"type": "text", "text": CLAUDE_CODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT});
```

This hard-codes Anthropic's identity-attestation string for OAuth model access. The doc-comment says "The Anthropic API validates this exact string". So `inject_system_prompt` lies to the upstream API about which client the request comes from — Claudia masquerades as Claude Code to gain access to subscription-gated models.

Two problems:
1. Architectural: a `claude_credentials` MODULE injects content into the SYSTEM PROMPT that the prompt-builder is unaware of. Block ordering and identity content are spread across `prompt.rs`, `claude_credentials.rs`, and `context.rs` with no single owner. The `inject_system_prompt` mutation also happens AFTER `build_system_prompt_blocks` has already produced a "stable_prefix" — so the cache marker on the original prefix block is invalidated by this prepend.
2. Operational: if Anthropic ever changes its OAuth identity-attestation string, this constant breaks silently and every user with an OAuth token sees 401s with no clear remediation path. The string is also undocumented in user-facing docs; users discovering it via grep will rightly wonder if they're violating terms of service.
## Mandated refactor
Move the OAuth prefix-block requirement into the prompt builder: `build_system_prompt_blocks` accepts an `Option<&str> oauth_prefix` parameter and emits it as block 0 when present. Delete `inject_system_prompt` and have the provider adapter (or the OAuth-authenticated path in `pipeline.rs`) supply the prefix at request construction time. Document the constant's purpose and origin in the doc comment with a link to Anthropic's policy.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
